race.name: Flyktningerennet
race.tagline: Forty-four kilometers from Norway to Sweden on the escape route refugees took in 1944 — a cross-border memorial race where every kilometer carries the weight of wartime history.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations along the cross-border route

climate.description: March in the Norwegian-Swedish border mountains means reliable winter conditions. The route crosses exposed mountain terrain between the two countries. Temperatures typically -10°C to -2°C. Snow reliability is high in this inland mountain zone.
challenges: Exposed mountain terrain on the border crossing
Variable conditions between sheltered valleys and open mountain

course.primary: Point-to-point from Nordli in Norway's Trøndelag region across the Scandinavian mountain border to Gäddede in Swedish Jämtland. The route follows the historic escape path used by Norwegian refugees fleeing to Sweden during WWII. Terrain mixes forested valleys with exposed mountain crossings at the border.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through mountain and forest terrain
course.grooming: Good Ski Classics Challengers standard on historic route
features: Follows the actual 1944 refugee escape route
Mix of Norwegian forest and Swedish mountain terrain
Start and finish in different countries

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Prestige boosted by 75 years of WWII memorial history — genuinely iconic heritage race. The cross-border format is unique in distance skiing. Community score reflects the deep emotional resonance of the event. Limited by remote location and modest field size.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score 60 >= 60 qualifies for TIER 2. Prestige 4 — already T2, no further promotion needed.

history.summary: Founded in 1950 to commemorate the flight of Norwegian refugees across the mountains to neutral Sweden during the German occupation in 1944. The 44km race follows the actual escape route from Nordli to Gäddede. It is one of Scandinavia's most historically significant ski races, carrying deep meaning for border communities. Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series.
notable_facts: Commemorates the 1944 refugee flight from Nazi-occupied Norway to Sweden
Founded just 5 years after the war's end, in 1950
The only major ski marathon that crosses an international border
Race name means 'The Refugee Race' in Norwegian
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
Start in Norway, finish in Sweden — two countries, one race
